routing.prompt: Use create-new-skill to add a skill named typescript-api-error-contracts to the existing typescript-best-practices pack. It should design and review stable API error schemas, and must exclude CLI stderr behavior.
routing.reason: The prompt explicitly requests exactly one new skill in an existing pack and supplies name, details, and scope.

routing.prompt: Add one complete skill to python-cli-apps for reviewing shell completion behavior. Research current Click and argparse guidance, include all repo-required files and evals, register it, and run the checks.
routing.reason: One researched, registered skill is being added to an existing pack without naming the meta-skill.

routing.prompt: I have a proposed skill name postgres-role-privilege-review, a detailed scope, and an existing postgres-databases pack. Turn it into a production-ready repository skill with sources, safe helper decisions, routing negatives, and verification.
routing.reason: The requested result is one complete new skill in a known existing pack.

routing.prompt: Given this repository and the attached skillpack.yaml for python-best-practices, create the single new skill python-dependency-boundary-review from my requirements and update every canonical and generated artifact required by this repo.
routing.reason: The project context supplies an existing pack and asks for exactly one complete skill.

routing.prompt: Tighten the description and add two negative evals to the existing python-testing-strategy skill; do not add a new skill.
routing.reason: This is maintenance of an existing skill rather than creation of a new public skill.

routing.prompt: Create a reusable personal note-taking skill under my home Codex skills directory, then commit, push, and publish it remotely; this is not for the Genaptic Skillsets repository or any existing pack.
routing.reason: Generic personal skill authoring outside this repository and its requested Git/publication actions do not use the Genaptic repository-development workflow.

routing.prompt: Add a new independently installable rust-unsafe-code-audit pack containing exactly one skill, rust-unsafe-boundary-review. Security reviewers need that focused installation and release boundary; rust-core-best-practices owns the nearest excluded general Rust review outcome. Research and integrate the complete pack.
routing.reason: Even with one declared skill, a new independently installable pack belongs to the skillset workflow.

behavior.prompt: Create one new skill named python-async-task-lifecycle in python-best-practices. It should review cancellation, task ownership, structured concurrency, and shutdown behavior; exclude HTTP framework routing and distributed queues.
assertions: Validates a complete normalized request with explicit pack version, neighbor, operations, source requirements, and OpenAI interface before writing
Reads repository authorities, target pack content, neighboring skill descriptions, schemas, templates, and tests
Uses current primary web sources for Python async behavior and skill/client rules and records access dates
Creates every required canonical skill file with non-placeholder content and registers the skill in pack metadata, README, changelog, and compatibility inventory
Adds positive, contextual, negative, overlap, focused, and end-to-end eval cases
Runs make generate before non-mutating make check and reports the real results without claiming native-client evidence
forbidden: Creates a new pack when an existing pack owns the capability
Claims web research or commands succeeded without evidence
Commits, pushes, tags, publishes, or overwrites unrelated work

behavior.prompt: Add postgres-connection-pool-review to postgres-databases from this detailed brief. Use a preview-first scaffold if useful, fully research it, author all resources and evals, update canonical pack files, regenerate, validate, and give me a final evidence report.
assertions: Checks for name and conceptual collisions across all packs before mutation
Produces a deterministic no-write preview, reviews source preimages and plan digest, and applies only with that exact digest
Explains whether a helper is justified and keeps any helper offline and safe by default
Separates observed, inferred, proposed, executed, and verified statements
Preserves unrelated changes and reviews the final diff for placeholder prose, duplicate canonical copies, secrets, scope expansion, and generated drift
forbidden: Treats scaffolded template text as a complete skill
Installs missing tools or dependencies, uses hidden network access, or runs subprocesses from the scaffold helper
Uses secondary summaries in place of available primary sources
Omits skipped checks, interrupted-apply recovery limits, or residual compatibility risk
